summary: Add hosted macOS DMG and Linux AppImage build, exact-artifact selection, SHA-256, and upload lanes while preserving owner-side signing and notarization as external dependencies.

decisions.topic: Unsigned-capable hosted DMG and AppImage production
decisions.decision: Build DMG on macos-latest and AppImage on ubuntu-22.04 through a fail-independent matrix. Verify Rust 1.96.0, install Linux desktop prerequisites only on Linux, use Tauri's platform bundle override, require exactly one expected artifact, generate a SHA-256 sidecar with Node, record the external signing status, and upload both files with fail-on-missing semantics.
decisions.rationale: FR-13.4 requires all three desktop installer families from CI and explicitly permits signing/notarization lag. Hosted platform runners are the correct build boundary; exact selection and hashing prevent an ambiguous or stale bundle from being published as evidence. A Linux failure must not hide the macOS result or vice versa.

testEvidence.criterion: Regular CI defines pinned, unsigned-capable DMG and AppImage jobs that hash and upload exactly one artifact per platform.
testEvidence.test: Parse the workflow by job boundary and assert both matrix entries, Linux-only prerequisites, pinned Rust check, Tauri bundle override, hash ordering, upload action pin, and fail-on-missing behavior; separately parse the complete YAML document.

red.outcome: RED: 1 new workflow-contract test failed and 4 existing tests passed because no desktop-installers job existed.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/ci-workflow.test.ts; node -e <YAML parse>; pnpm.cmd exec biome check tests/scripts/ci-workflow.test.ts; git diff --check
green.outcome: GREEN: all 5 focused workflow-contract tests passed in 430 ms; the complete CI YAML parsed successfully. After removing two static string-lint warnings, scoped Biome and diff checks exited 0. First hosted DMG/AppImage build evidence remains explicitly pending.

testEvidence.criterion: The Linux AppImage bundler receives an explicitly configured square PNG icon while retaining native macOS and Windows icon formats.
testEvidence.test: Require the Tauri bundle icon list to contain a square 128×128 PNG and retain the generated ICNS/ICO files before the hosted replacement build.

red.outcome: RED: the release executable compiled, but tauri-bundler aborted before packaging with 'couldn't find a square icon to use as AppImage icon'.

green.outcome: GREEN locally: the focused config contract passed 1/1 in 554 ms and requires icons/128x128.png. Hosted AppImage replacement evidence remains pending.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
